game:theatre:act1-the-pager:act1-cant-le.note: [player] This feels dumb. When you try to go south it says 'you're not leaving without your pager.' This is a hint in search of a puzzle. We should avoid dumb hints, err towards NOT providing a hint — less hints, less clues, less text.
game:theatre:act1-the-pager:act1-cant-le.hintText: Why did you come back into this building in the first place?
game:theatre:act1-the-pager:act1-cant-le.detail: Dropped the Socratic rung 1 — the game already states the pager is in the basement, so the puzzle is trivial (rule 19 / rule 14). Ladder now: basement nudge → answer.

game:bronze:getting-around:reading-refer.hintText: They aren't just scenery — this castle will answer questions, if you know how to ask.
game:bronze:getting-around:reading-refer.detail: Kept the entry — it's a mechanic primer teaching the LOOK UP <topic> IN <book> verb that most hard Bronze puzzles depend on (not a fake puzzle). Softened rung 1 to read as a plain queryable-books tip rather than a riddle hook.

game:anchorhead:day-one:d1-office:2.note: [player] Don't need this '— and look up, not just around.' Should be trimming harder.
game:anchorhead:day-one:d1-office:2.hintText: Try the alley around the side of the building — and look up, not just around.
game:anchorhead:day-one:d1-office:2.detail: Cut the '— and look up, not just around.' clause (rule-14 over-delivery: it leaked rung 3's transom-window reveal). Rung now: 'Try the alley around the side of the building.'

game:anchorhead:day-two:d2-paintings:2.note: [player] I like pure atmosphere, but I don't understand the last line at all ('The presence or absence...') — you can prob cut that. The hint basically says it all anyway.
game:anchorhead:day-two:d2-paintings:2.hintText: Answer: Pure atmosphere — the paintings do nothing you can act on. Look them over for the family's faces and names if you like, but there's no puzzle here. The presence or absence of a question is no guide to what matters.
game:anchorhead:day-two:d2-paintings:2.detail: Deleted the whole d2-paintings entry (not just the trailing line). An 'is this important? → no, pure atmosphere' question serves no stuck player and only enlarges the spoiler surface. Hardened generate-hints rule 16 with this as the canonical Bad example: red herrings must protect or entertain, never be an earnest 'it's nothing' deflation.

game:wishbringer:festeron:fest-errand:3.note: [player] 'Fairy-tale shop' is weird — it's just a magick shop or some other kind of shop, right? The 'pick up any loose objects' is a totally unrelated factoid and should have been caught in review as needless.
game:wishbringer:festeron:fest-errand:3.hintText: From the western edge of the village, follow the winding cliffside path and keep heading up; the little fairy-tale shop sits near the top. Pick up any loose objects you pass on the way.
game:wishbringer:festeron:fest-errand:3.detail: Cut the 'Pick up any loose objects' sentence (rule-14 tail, duplicates fest-gather) and reworded 'the little fairy-tale shop' → 'a little shop' (the name waits for the answer rung).

game:wishbringer:festeron:fest-errand:1.note: [player] The boss handed a letter to deliver, right? Not a package?
game:wishbringer:festeron:fest-errand:1.hintText: There is a rush. No puzzle is blocking you yet — you're a clerk and this is simply a delivery — but the place you're delivering to closes at nightfall, and you do not want to be out on the streets after dark.
game:wishbringer:festeron:fest-errand:1.detail: Factual fix: question said 'package' but the answer rung and in-game item are an envelope. Changed the question to 'an envelope to deliver.'
